Full provider setup needed. Acceptance: a) auth-profiles chmod 600, b) backup excludes auth files, c) Ollama Cloud provider configured, d) CI Cycle A reverted to deepseek-pro.

C: Ollama Cloud models added to models.providers.ollama — kimi-k2.6, deepseek-v4-pro, deepseek-v4-flash (CHG-0155). D: CI Cycle A reverted to deepseek-v4-pro:cloud (CHG-0155). E: cron-agent-preflight.sh updated with correct cloud model resolution logic — 5/5 crons PASS. HTTP API verified working via local Ollama Pro.

tickets.title: Content gating bypass — W1P1+ cron posts without confirmed Ken approval
tickets.description: Approval flow loophole: week1-posts.md status='Pending Ken approval' but W1P1/W1P2/W1P3 crons fire directly to LinkedIn API without checking approval state. Ken must reply APPROVED:[queue-id] per SPARK_RULES.md but no gate enforces this before posting. Risk: unapproved content published automatically. Fix: crons must check linkedin-queue.json for status=approved before posting, halt and alert if not.
tickets.resolution: Approval gate added to all 3 W1P crons (Step 0). Gate reads linkedin-queue.json, checks status=approved for the contentId, halts and alerts Ken via Telegram if not approved. Queue created with LI-W1-P1/P2/P3 all set to approved (Ken approved 2026-05-04 15:17 AEST).

tickets.title: Nexus P1-P4 Roadmap — EA technology roadmap, blueprint and checklist

ATLAS NOTE (2026-05-05): Ensure the P1-P4 roadmap includes a dedicated Technology Availability & Stability layer covering: (1) System availability architecture per phase — HA design, failover, recovery targets (RTO/RPO); (2) Observability stack — event loop monitoring, zombie task detection, gateway health, agent health, model performance trending; (3) AIOps maturity model — what checks exist per phase, what gets automated vs human-escalated; (4) Infrastructure resilience patterns — OC1/OC2 HA, Tailscale mesh, NAS failover; (5) SLA/SLO/error budget design per phase (P1 internal vs P2 client-facing vs P3/P4 enterprise); (6) Incident response capability per phase — runbooks, on-call model, escalation paths. Trigger: today's zombie task + event loop incident (INC CHG-0180) exposed gaps in our AIOps coverage. This layer must be a first-class section in the EA roadmap, not an afterthought.

tickets.title: SRE Agent — Krennic 🔵 (Site Reliability Engineering)
tickets.description: Build Krennic — SRE agent for AInchors platform. Owns incident response, SLO/error budget management, runbook execution, post-mortems, capacity planning, and toil reduction. Must be live BEFORE TRIGGER-07 (first P2 client). P1: design + spec. P2: activate. Activation also triggers if internal incident rate >2/week OR toil >30% of Yoda turns. Named after Director Orson Krennic — dedication, precision, uncompromising standards. Star Wars principle: commitment and craft, not allegiance.

tickets.title: Audit Log Architecture — Beacon v2: structured trace ID, tool-call logging, unified audit spine
tickets.description: P2 blocker. Gap from AI governance review (2026-05-05). Current state: two separate systems — tech logs (gateway.err.log) and audit logs (CHANGELOG.md + obs.db + Notion) — no bridge, no shared trace ID, no tool-call structured records, no prompt/response logging. Required: (1) Shared trace ID across multi-step agent runs. (2) Structured tool-call audit records: timestamp, tool name, params summary, outcome. (3) Policy event log: which guardrail fired, allow/block/modify decision, why. (4) Gateway + orchestrator + connector three-layer logging spine. (5) Same spine serves operational debugging AND compliance/regulatory reporting. Design owner: Atlas (EA) + Thrawn (PA). Build owner: Forge + Krennic (when live). Must be complete before P2 client onboarding.

tickets.title: AI Governance Framework v1.1 — S4 resolved, HITL thresholds, gateway PII control as P2 gate
tickets.description: Three updates required from gap analysis (2026-05-05): (1) S4 status — update YODA NOTE item 1 from 'known gap' to resolved. Document the tool scope matrix implemented CHG-0175 (2026-05-05). (2) HITL threshold table — add concrete triggers per domain to Charter (monetary thresholds, data-access triggers, new provider triggers). Currently qualitative tiers only — not sufficient for P2 audit. (3) Gateway-level PII control — add as a P2 pre-condition: lightweight automated PII pattern detection at the gateway layer before external API calls. Currently enforcement is config-level only (model routing). Infrastructure-level enforcement gap. Lex to assess and propose. Owner: Yoda. Requires Lex + Sage review before v1.1 approved.

tickets.title: Persistent Agent Configuration — Stateless Bootstrap
tickets.description: All rules, routing policies, and configurations that agents need to operate must be persistent and stateless. Nothing critical should exist only in Gateway runtime state or agent memory. Everything must be written to files in the workspace repo, bootstrapped upon agent load, or validated/restored by auto-heal/PVT. Triggered by: Telegram routing misconfiguration (2026-05-06) where a cron delivery accountId setting had no durable enforcement until post-incident. Scope: audit all agent operational dependencies, define bootstrap manifest, implement load-time validation.
tickets.notes: AC12 added 2026-05-07: Create dedicated agent directories for Shield, Lex, and Sage with proper SOUL.md and rules files. G1-G2 governance guardrails currently in governance-guardrails-2026-05.md only — need proper agent dirs for full separation. Tag: persistent-config, governance, stateless-bootstrap.

tickets.title: Strategy coherence review, governance gap analysis, Atlas tech roadmap P1-P5, backlog replan, Agile framework lock
tickets.description:
5-step governance and strategy alignment sequence:
Step 1 (Coherence): Review loaded strategy MDs (VMS, OKR 2026-05, Guardrails 2026-05) for internal consistency, conflicting instructions, gaps.
Step 2 (Governance): Review Step 1 output against existing governance/policies (AI Charter, AI Governance Framework, S1-S7, agent rules). Identify gaps, loose threads, realignment needs.
Step 3 (Atlas): Ken uploads tech strategy MD. Atlas produces: current + target state architecture roadmap, P1-P5 transition states (progressive delivery — no over-engineering), immediate 1Y plan and priorities.
Step 4 (Backlog replan): Run Step 3 output against existing backlog frameworks. Replan and reprioritise backlog.
Step 5 (TRIGGER): On completion of Step 4 — lock AGILE delivery framework: QBR cadence, epic scope, sprint scope. Non-negotiable gate.
Reconstructed 2026-05-07 after 3AM session context loss (pre-empted by CHG-0215 Decision Capture Rule).

tickets.title: OC2 deployment playbook in Holocron — TRIGGER-01 pre-documentation (Atlas Q1 Must-Do #5)
tickets.description: Atlas Q1 Must-Do #5. Document TRIGGER-01 sequence in Holocron before OC2 arrives July 2026. HIVE deployment cannot be improvised. Covers: OC2-A/B setup, Ollama install, Gemma4:26b load, OpenClaw config, Tailscale mesh, failover test procedure. OKR: X1-KR4.
